title: PolicyDocument
description: Schema for the AI Agent Assembly PolicyDocument v1. Defines the governance rules applied to an agent by aa-gateway. The requires_approval_if expression language is not specified in v1 of this schema — expression syntax is deferred to the policy engine implementation (AAASM-8).

network.description: Network access controls for the agent.

allowlist.description: List of domain glob patterns the agent is permitted to reach (e.g. '*.openai.com', 'api.github.com'). Requests to domains not matching any pattern are denied.

tools.description: Per-tool governance rules. Keys are tool names as reported by the agent (e.g. 'web_search', 'code_interpreter'). Tools not listed here inherit the gateway default policy.

additionalProperties.description: Governance rule for a single tool.

allow.description: Whether this tool is permitted to be called. Set to false to block the tool outright.

limit_per_hour.description: Maximum number of calls to this tool allowed per hour. Calls beyond this limit are denied until the next hour window.

requires_approval_if.description: Condition expression that triggers a human-in-the-loop approval workflow before the tool call is dispatched. Syntax is TBD and will be defined by the policy engine (AAASM-8). Any non-empty string is accepted at the schema validation level.

data.description: Data sensitivity controls applied to all agent inputs and outputs.

sensitive_patterns.description: List of regular expression patterns used to detect sensitive data (e.g. PII, credentials). Any match in an agent's input or output triggers the configured redaction or denial action.

budget.description: Spending budget controls for LLM API calls made by the agent.

daily_limit_usd.description: Maximum USD spend allowed per calendar day across all LLM API calls. Calls that would exceed this limit are denied. Must be greater than 0.

schedule.description: Time-based access controls that restrict when the agent is permitted to operate.

active_hours.description: The time window during which the agent is allowed to run. Requests outside this window are denied.

start.description: Start of the active window in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g. '09:00').

end.description: End of the active window in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g. '18:00'). Must be after start.

timezone.description: IANA timezone name for the active_hours window (e.g. 'Asia/Taipei', 'America/New_York', 'UTC').
